feed.title: SAP Community - SAP BW/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP BW/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Value is not coming for a new node in rsrt
entry.content:
Hi All,

I created a new Node in the BEx as shown below

with the below selection details

But in RSRT screen only this columns is refelecting but no data is coming although, there is data available in the ACDOCA table shown in the below screenshot.

 

Kindly help me with this issue.

Regards,

Saima Afreen

entry.title: Percentage Key Figure
entry.content:
How to calculate a percentage key figure in BW query?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Unable to write select query in BW transformations
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I want to display current date in one of the field routine of the BW transformations. I wrote the below code in field routine and getting the error message as
Incorrect syntax error near :CUR_DATE.

outtab = select (select current_date into CUR_DATE from dummy AS "CUR_DATE"),
"RECORD",
"SQL__PROCEDURE__SOURCE__RECORD"
from :INTAB;

Please guide me how to solve this error.

Thanks.

entry.title: Error Switching to B4H Mode: "Unexpected Situation Occurred. Please Contact SAP"
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently encountering an issue when attempting to switch our operating mode to B4H. Despite ensuring that all prerequisite tasks have been completed, we consistently receive the following error message: "Unexpected situation occurred. Please contact SAP."

Has anyone else faced this problem before? If so, how were you able to resolve it? Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated as we are trying to troubleshoot this issue but are unsure what might be causing it.

Thank you in advance for your help and support!

Best regards,

Jonas

entry.title: SAP Field service management to BW/4Hana
entry.content:
Hi all, 
I am looking for infomation on connection SAP FSM to BW/4HANA or Datasphere.
Does anybody has experiences with that or can give some hints.

Thanks Dirk

entry.title: BW HANA Sizing - Results interpretation /SDF/HANA_BW_SIZING
entry.content:
How should the results of the report /SDF/HANA_BW_SIZING be interpreted? is the recommended physical memory per node the final "HANA DB size" also displayed in the Readiness check report? It is very confusing as the sum of the memory 838 GB + data 1271 GB + log 512 GB disk size, is not equal to the recommended physical memory per node (1536 GB). 

Thanks for any clarification.

entry.title: What are the best approach to store/replicate historical data and when Delta merge fails? (SLT)
entry.content: Currently we are replicating data form S/4 to BW/4 HANA system using SLT(using Delta merge). But we are facing issue while replicating ACDOCA data due to huge volume of data (using SLT Delta Merge). What are the best approaches to store/replicate historical data and when Delta merge fails

entry.title: How to display the text of InfoObjects of ADSO in HANA *8 View comments ?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

     The text of InfoObject G0COMPANY is "G0COMPANY" in BW ADSO ZADSO_001. 

    But the corresponding HANA View /BIC/AZADSO_0018(*8 View) has no comment of field.

 

This is the system design or a bug ?

Tks.

entry.title: SAP BW: Automatic sending of server files (al11) to a shared Windows directory
entry.content:
Hi Guys!,

I have created a data model whose data source is a query that is loaded into an open hub which generates a .csv output file in a directory on the al11 transaction server.
And I would need to send this file to another server to deposit it in a Windows directory.
Do you know how I could configure the sending of the file in BW?

BR,

entry.title: Multiple Delta - Delta with different selection on 0FI_ACDOCA_10
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have below scenario:

ACDOCA has lot of volume and even the delta volume goes high during the period end due to the balance carry forward program. Due to the high volume the jobs run for long time and failed twice. 

We were thinking of creating multiple INIT with company code selection for 0FI_ACDOCA_10 so that the volume is split across company codes. When we trigger first init and delta for company code 1111 it get the correct data. Next we do a init and delta for 2222. When we check the records in ODQMON for 2222, it get the data for 1111 as well. Same when another delta is trigged for 1111 it get data for 2222 as well. 

Please let me know if these multiple init with different selection is not possible for 0FI_ACDOCA_10 extractor.

We did do the similar task for 0TE_OBJ_STATUS_HIST. Exiting delta was working for 3 OBTYP - object category and we were requested for additional 5 OBTYP - object category. We just did a new init/delta for 5 new object category and all worked fine.

Please let me know if multiple delta with different selection will work for 0FI_ACDOCA_10.

Thanks,

Vinay

entry.title: Web Dispatcher Version showing as out of date
entry.content:
Hi all

 

I'm trying to understand if the Web Dispatcher is still a product that should be used. We recently updated to one of the latest versions (789) but if you go to the software documentation it shows the following URL which is deprecated.

https://help.sap.com/viewer/683d6a1797a34730a6e005d1e8de6f22/latest/en-US/488fe37933114e6fe10000000a421937.html

entry.title: BW aDSO - activate table changes
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

We've got a list of aDSO's which are used for customizing tables in planning applications or as mapping tables. The request from the business/external auditors is now to provide log of table changes for that tables, so that evaluation can be done by table DBTABLOG and its standard transaction SCU3.

We thought we could activate option "Log Changes" by transaction SE13 for /BIC/A...2 tables in our development system. When we did that test, we faced the issue that change is not added to transport. So we activated aDSO by Eclipse and by that transport entry was created. But after that option "Log Changes" is deactivated again.

In Prod system we are not able to change that option, because of mandant status.

How to activate "Log Changes" option for aDSO's to provide external auditors table log?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: MS Excel From SAP
entry.content:
Using the Excel - Data - From Database - From SAP HANA Database interface
in Excel MS 365 V.2308  1673.20674

The Infoobject Equipment is setup in BW with Text but only parts of the values are delivered with a text depending on the position in the Technical Place hierarchy.
For certain object it does not make sense to have a text and in the BW Reporting environment it is no problem.

But with this interface it seems to be a serious since the default display is set to the infoobject text.

As soon as there is no text, the value NULL appears instead of the key as eg. in AfO.
Since by SAP standards the text does not have to be unique (and is not checked for) the display of the text only is also not feasible since a unique selection is not possible.

Question:

Is there a way to setup either 2 columns (key/text) or display the key instead of the missing text?

With this setting the values with missing text are virtually not existing and therefore make the report/interface technical/logically incorrect  and therefor worthless.

The known ressources on that topic go in on any possible detail but that rather basic aspect.

entry.title: Is anybody loading data from FSM Cloud into BW4HANA?
entry.content:
Aside from the inevitable ODATA connection, we are using that for C4C and its by all means horrible (slow, dropouts, no delta, maybe due to old version => for months pending OSS),

we still have hope there is something less fancy modern but decently working that idealy enables hourly deltas 24/7.

Thanks for Help

Dirk

entry.title: Oracle tables not available in BW/4HANA(under HANA_SDA option) post connection.
entry.content:
Hi Experts, we have connected Oracle system to BW/4HANA system via SDA. However, the table under the desired schema isn't visible. In RSA1, all the system connection looks fine. What could be the possible reason? Your help is greatly appreciated.

Thank You.

#SDA #BW/4HANA #Oracledatabase

entry.title: Filter Section : Fixed Values vs Default values. #BW4HANA #BEX
entry.content:
Hi Everybody,

I'm trying to filter my query but I found two panels in BW4HANA.

What is the difference between Filter Fixed Values and Filter Default values? In the older version, we are aware of selecting the Default value. But not completely sure why we have two filter panels?? In which scenario do we go with them??

I tried to filter with values on my material in default and fixed panel and I found there is not difference.

Can someone please help me on it.

#BW4HANA  #BEX  #BW

Thank you.

Kind Regards,

V

entry.title: DTP step stuck at Extract. from Datastore step in Data package
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are performing a delta data load from one ADSO to another ADSO and this runs like 8 times everyday. But during one of the loads the DTP is taking more time at Extract. from Datastore step in the Data Package. There is code in start and end routine, but it is taking time at the start only. 

The system has ample work processes available, and the tables used in the code for lookup doesn't have any load or activation while the actual load is in progress. We even got the trace enable and notice it is taking more time at the step DB:open.

We have 4 app servers and have allocated 25 work processes for this DTP. only thing is that there are multiple (around 4-5) activation jobs each having allocated 25 WPs trigger during this load. The load used to take 35-40 mins earlier is now taking 80-100 mins with no increase in data volume.

Any insights on this issue will be really helpful. 

Regards,

Srinivas

entry.title: Exporting BEx Queries from BW on HANA to BW4HANA??
entry.content:
Has anybody recently been involved with migration from BW on HANA to BW4HANA?

I want to know how you met the challenge of migrating BEx queries to BW4HANA.

Are there any new tools available? If not what is the least complicated way to migrate.

If there is no SAP tool is anybody aware of any third-party tool.

I need this information urgently, and any information will be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Broadcasting SAP BW Analysis for Office report
entry.content: I am looking to broadcast SAP BW Analysis for Office report, Is it possible? If yes, please guide.

entry.title: SAP BW/4HANA Data Model for SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting
entry.content:
Co-Authored: Andreas Tenholte and Philip Fengler

Introduction

We in the CoE EMEA Team for Data Warehousing & Data Management have developed a Group Reporting data model for SAP BW/4HANA. This model is now out of the pilot phase and is already being used successfully by several customers.

In the following, we would like to give you a brief overview of the model's target audience, its structure and functionality, as well as information on how to get access to the model if you are interested. So stay tuned, it's worth reading 😊

 

Target Group for the SAP BW/4HANA Group Reporting Model

A data model for SAP BW/4HANA has been requested by several customers who have one or more of the following requirements:

  • Use of SAP BW/4HANA as the central reporting platform for the execution of financial consolidation reporting.
  • The need to combine group reporting data with analytical data such as HCM KPIs, production data, etc.
  • Use the flexible analysis authorization framework to fine-tune row-level access based on hierarchy nodes such as consolidation unit and profit center.
  • The need to load and distribute group reporting data to other systems.

 

Interrelation between SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting and the BW Group Reporting Model

The provided SAP BW/4HANA data model is implemented based on the data structures used in SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting and leverages standard CDS views delivered as part of SAP S/4HANA, to extract and load the data into the receiving data model in SAP BW/4HANA. This data is then made available for reporting by applying group reporting logic including hierarchy elimination logic.

 

About the SAP BW/4HANA Group Reporting Model

The SAP BW/4HANA data model consists of Z*-objects (BW objects, ABAP CDS Views, HANA Calculation Views), covering transactional and master data. The model is aligned with SAP best practices, such as “Layered-Scalable-Architecture optimized for HANA” (LSA++) and HANA-optimized business content. The BW data model is currently not part of the official SAP BW/4 Business Content. This aspect requires further elaboration.

The following Group Reporting functionality is covered by the data model:

  • Version relations including extension versions
  • Group derivation logic based on the group structure
  • Hierarchy elimination
  • Period mode

The reports that can be created using the SAP BW/4HANA data model for Group Reporting provide the same functionality as the SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics reports of Group Data Analysis.

It is also possible to extend the functional scope of the Group Reporting data model to meet individual business requirements as part of the service delivery (see below):

  • Downporting the SAP BW/4HANA data model to SAP BW on HANA 7.50
  • Reporting rules to cover Cash Flow reporting, that corresponds to Group Data Analysis including Reporting Rules in SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics
  • Handling of physical deletions

 

How to get access to the SAP BW/4HANA Group Reporting Model?

The service delivery is carried out by the CoE EMEA Team for Data Warehousing and Data Management as part of the Premium Engagement. 

Below is an overview of the service delivery plan and the added value of the collaboration as part of the Premium Engagement.

Service Plan Proposal

  • Enablement Workshop for Data and Analytics is the service to get to understand the SAP BW/4HANA pilot data model and, based on customer-specific business demands, identify potential gaps resulting in a high-level design and project plan for the proof-of-concept phase (Jump Start Service).
  • Jump-start for Analytics and Data Management to deploy the pilot data model for SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting in customers SAP BW/4HANA sandbox system with the adjustments as scoped, testing, final sign-off including hand-over to the project team.
  • Safeguarding Services, on Performance Optimization (BPPO, TPO), Quality Assurance Assessments (FTC, TIC), Going Live Support, etc. complete the service plan proposal.

Value

  • Speed up Time-to-Value: Fast deployment of a proven pilot data model for SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting with SAP BW/4HANA.
  • Know-how-transfer how the data model works including key design considerations.
  • Utilize the know-how of SAP experts to reduce the TCO of the application model for Group Reporting in SAP BW/4HANA e.g., by understanding Data Tiering Optimization options.
  • Option to extend and enrich the model based on customers specific requirements for Group Reporting by the input and help of SAP experts.

If you are interested in getting in touch with us, you will find the necessary contacts in our Focus Package.
